title: Coreosma sanguinea
intro: Coreosma sanguinea, also known as the 'Blood-flowered Coreosma', is a flowering plant species within the family Lamiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Coreosma sanguinea include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coreosma sanguinea is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and sometimes used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and syrups for various health conditions.
taxonomy: Coreosma sanguinea belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant within the genus Coreosma. Coreosma sanguinea is native to Australia and is often classified as a shrub. The classification of Coreosma sanguinea is typically categorized as a member of the Angiospermophyta division and further divided into the subclass Asteridae. Further taxonomic classification places Coreosma sanguinea within the order Gentianales.
uses: Coreosma sanguinea is a species of flowering plant that has been traditionally used in medicine in various parts of the world. Its medicinal uses include treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as being used to manage menstrual disorders.
